design_philosophy.core_loop: Execute varied ninja-cyborg attacks and weapon combos to defeat enemies while navigating platforming challenges across short, checkpoint-sparse stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited lives, no saves, few checkpoints) versus execution demands (pattern recognition and precise timing in combat and platforming)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Vengeful ninja-cyborg hybrid combining traditional Japanese ninjutsu aesthetics with futuristic cybernetic power to destroy an evil opposing clan
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising arcade-style design: no save system, limited continues (7 in JP), short stages with minimal checkpoints, and permadeath loss of health power-ups. Blends classical ninja/samurai aesthetics with sci-fi cyborg protagonist—a deliberate aesthetic hybrid. Emphasizes mastery of a wide moveset (somersaults, wall-jumps, charge attacks, weapon switching) over narrative progression

difficulty.lives_system: Three hit-points baseline; health power-ups can increase meter but are lost on death. Running out of lives forces restart of current chapter
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues: seven in Japanese version before game over. Continue places player at chapter start
difficulty.checkpointing: Very few checkpoints across stages; stages are notably short but sparse in save points
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited. Health pickups provide some mitigation, but no difficulty select, no save system, and rigid continue limits create high stakes. Reviewers noted 'frustrating platforming sequences' indicating difficulty spikes

progression.upgrade_system: Health power-ups that increase player meter beyond base three hits; magic attacks with limited availability (screen-clearing utility)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Four weapons available for switching (sword, chain, shuriken, grenades); unlocked contextually through stages rather than progression tree
replayability_hooks: Mastery of weapon variety and move combos incentivizes replays
Arcade-style high-score/completion drive (no saves create 'one-run' mentality)
One stage features unique Mode 7 hovercraft sequence, breaking formula

world_building_techniques: Plot explains two rival ninja clans (Fuma and Koma) with faction hierarchies based on Chinese zodiac and color coding
Holy Grail as MacGuffin driving conflict
Hagane's cyborg resurrection and revenge motivation frames the entire campaign
Ending shows bittersweet death/acceptance: Hagane's eyes fade, nature reclaims the corrupted landscape—environmental storytelling in finale

faction_indicators: Fuma clan: zodiac-based factions, protectors of Holy Grail, spiritual strength
Koma clan: color-coded factions (white, gold, red dragon), plotters to destroy world
Clan isolation: bloodlines cut off from outside, members ignorant of sister factions
danger_signals: Holy Grail theft by evil Koma clan signals existential threat
Hagane as sole survivor represents last hope—lone-wolf vengeance narrative
Cyborg resurrection blurs humanity, foreshadows sacrifice/death
environmental_storytelling: Futuristic setting blended with traditional Japanese architecture and aesthetics
Mode 7 hovercraft sequence signals technological civilization
Final image: nature reclaiming blackened land, rust consuming Hagane's katana—cyclical decay and acceptance of mortality
